intro: Armenia (from the Hebrew Ararat) was the highland region north and northeast of Assyria corresponding to the modern Armenian plateau. The Authorized Version uses Armenia where the Revised Version reads Ararat—notably in 2 Kings 19:37 (= Isaiah 37:38), where the sons of Sennacherib fled there after murdering their father, and in Jeremiah 51:27, where it is called to arms against Babylon. Genesis 8:4 records that Noah's ark rested on the mountains of Ararat. The name of the ancient kingdom of Urartu, centered on Lake Van, underlies both terms.
